---
output:
  html_document: 
    self_contained: yes
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_collapsed: true
    number_sections: true
    theme: cosmo
params:
  phenotype_name: "phenotype of interest"
  color_code_yaml: NA
  metaxcan_dir: NA
  smultixcan_file: NA
  smetaboxcan_file: NA
  predict_db: NA
  gene_metabolite_assoc_file: NA
  summary_stat_file: NA
  prefix: NA
  gene_annotation: NA
  mqtl_db: NA
  metabo_pathways: NA
  ld_blocks: NA
  liftover_chain: NA
  omics_network: NA
  gene_info: NA
  out_prefix: NA
  rlib: NA
  ntop: 20
  sample_size: NA
  h2: NA
  output_dir: NA
title: "`r paste0('MetaboXcan results for ', params$phenotype_name)`"
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
suppressMessages(library(tidyverse))
suppressMessages(library(tools))
suppressMessages(library(ggplot2))
suppressMessages(library(pander))
suppressMessages(library(kableExtra))
suppressMessages(library(ggfastman))
suppressMessages(library(liftOver))
suppressMessages(library(GenomicRanges))
suppressMessages(library(reticulate))
suppressMessages(library(cowplot))
suppressMessages(library(knitr))
suppressMessages(library(data.table))
suppressMessages(library(patchwork))
suppressMessages(library(RSQLite))
suppressMessages(library(igraph))
suppressMessages(library(visNetwork))
suppressMessages(library(DT))
suppressMessages(library(openxlsx))
suppressMessages(library(rprojroot))
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#use_python("/Users/festo/miniconda3/envs/bioinf/bin/python3")
use_condaenv(condaenv = 'metaboxcan', required = TRUE)
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 12))
theme_set(theme_cowplot())
panderOptions('table.split.table', Inf)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

```{r parameters}
metaxcan_dir = params$metaxcan_dir
smultixcan_file = params$smultixcan_file
smetaboxcan_file = params$smetaboxcan_file
gene_metabolite_assoc_file = params$gene_metabolite_assoc_file
phenotype_name = params$phenotype_name
prefix_name = params$phenotype_name
ntop = params$ntop
sample_size = params$sample_size
h2 = params$h2
summary_stat_file = params$summary_stat_file
annotation_file = params$gene_annotation
metabolite_qtls_file = params$mqtl_db
predict_db = params$predict_db
pathways_file = params$metabo_pathways
ldblocks_file = params$ld_blocks
l_chain = params$liftover_chain
out_dir = params$output_dir
omics_network = params$omics_network
gene_info = params$gene_info
```


# The OmicXcan Workflow
This workflow is used to find the associations between genes, metabolite and complex traits

```{r out-fig,dpi = 100, out.width="99%", fig.height=5}
fig_path <- normalizePath(current_input())
knitr::include_graphics(glue::glue("./metabo_workflow.png"))
#devtools::source_gist("38431b74c6c0bf90c12f")
```

```{r}
# helpers
# function to add cytoband genes
addband2gene = function(df,geneid = "ensembl_gene_id") {
  if(!exists("anno_gene")) {
    ensembl <- biomaRt::useMart(biomart="ENSEMBL_MART_ENSEMBL",
                                dataset="hsapiens_gene_ensembl")
    anno_gene <- biomaRt::getBM(attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id","external_gene_name",
                                            "chromosome_name","start_position",
                                            "end_position","band", "gene_biotype"), mart=ensembl)
  #print("defined anno_gene")
  }
  if(geneid != "ensembl_gene_id") names(df)[names(df)==geneid] = "ensembl_gene_id" ## this is an ugly workaround - need to find a way to use rename_ for this but don't know how to specify a string instead of name in rename(geneid = "ensembl_gene_id")
  df = df %>% left_join(anno_gene, by=c( "ensembl_gene_id"))
  if(geneid != "ensembl_gene_id") names(df)[names(df)=="ensembl_gene_id"] = geneid
  df %>% mutate(cytoband = paste0(chromosome_name,band))
}

# define the liftover function
my_liftover <- function(df,chain_file){
  # load the chain
  chain <- import.chain(chain_file)
  # make the gr co ordinates
  df <- df %>% 
    dplyr::rename(seqname=chr,start=pos) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(end = start)
  gr <- makeGRangesFromDataFrame(df, TRUE)
  gr <- liftOver(gr, chain)
  
  new_df <- as.data.table(gr) %>%
      dplyr::select(-c(group,group_name,width,strand)) %>% 
      dplyr::rename(chr=seqnames,pos=start) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(chr = as.character(chr))
  return(new_df)
}

# genome builds 
# genes GTEx hg38
# metabos hg38

```


```{r load_dir, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# load the data silently
# all gene tissue association files
s_files <- list.files(path = metaxcan_dir, 
                      pattern = glue::glue("{prefix_name}_GT_en_.*\\.csv$"), full.names = T)
# multixcan results
multixcan <- fread(smultixcan_file)
multixcan <- multixcan %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(t_i_best = str_remove(t_i_best, "en_"))
# metaboxcan results
metaboxcan <- fread(smetaboxcan_file) %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(pvalue))
# gene, metabo assoc file
gene_metabolite_df <- fread(gene_metabolite_assoc_file)
# metabolite annotation file
paths_db <- fread(pathways_file)
# network files
m.network <- fread(omics_network)
gene_info <- fread(gene_info)
```


# Manhattan plot for associated genes, metabolites and the trait
For each gene associated with `r phenotype_name`, eqtls are fetched from the GTEx portal and used to generate the manhattan plot. For each metabolite associated with `r phenotype_name` significantly (10^-3) associated mqtls are fetched and used to generate the manhattan plot. 
```{r get_sig_genes, echo=FALSE}
sig_gene_df <- multixcan %>% 
  dplyr::filter(pvalue < 0.05/nrow(multixcan)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene,gene_name,pvalue,z_mean,t_i_best) 

if (nrow(sig_gene_df) == 0) {
  print(paste0("NB: No Significant association at pvalue of ",0.05/nrow(multixcan),"(bonferroni sig). We are selecting the top 100 genes associations only."))
  sig_gene_df <- multixcan %>% 
    dplyr::select(gene,gene_name,pvalue,z_mean,t_i_best) %>% 
    slice_min(pvalue, n = 100)
}

sig_genes <- sig_gene_df %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene,gene_name,t_i_best,pvalue)
```

```{python get-eqtls_gtex,echo=FALSE}
import requests
import json
import pandas as pd 

to_fetch = r.sig_genes
#to_fetch.head()
gw_df = pd.DataFrame() 
for index, row in to_fetch.iterrows():
  #print(row['gene'], row['t_i_best'])
  gencode = row['gene']
  gene_name = row['gene_name']
  tissue = row['t_i_best']
  # portal changed from https://gtexportal.org/rest/v1/association/singleTissueEqtl
  # doc: https://gtexportal.org/api/v2/docs
  gene_eqtls = requests.get('https://gtexportal.org/api/v2/association/singleTissueEqtl', 
                   params = {"gencodeId" : gencode,"tissueSiteDetailId" : tissue, 
                   "datasetId" : "gtex_v8"})
  data = json.loads(gene_eqtls.text)
  if 'data' in data.keys():
    myqtl = pd.json_normalize(data['data'])
  else:
    myqtl = pd.DataFrame()
    #print(f"Cannot find the singleTissueEqtl from multixcan({tissue}) for {gene_name} gene")
    
  if myqtl.empty:
    #print(f"Trying to get eqtls from all tissues as the best tissue from multixcan({tissue}) for {gene_name} gene has none")
    gene_eqtls = requests.get('https://gtexportal.org/api/v2/association/singleTissueEqtl', 
                   params = {"gencodeId" : gencode, "datasetId" : "gtex_v8"})
    data = json.loads(gene_eqtls.text)
    myqtl = pd.json_normalize(data['data'])
  gw_df = gw_df.append(myqtl,ignore_index=True)

# gw_df.head()
```

```{r format_eqtl,echo=FALSE}
gw_eqtls <- py$gw_df %>% dplyr::select(snpId,chromosome,pos,pValue,geneSymbol) %>% 
  dplyr::rename(rsid=snpId,chr=chromosome,pvalue=pValue) %>% 
  mutate(rsid = sapply(rsid, toString)) %>% 
  mutate(study_id="snp2gene")

# Format and add colour for each gene
gw_eqtls$color <- as.character(factor(gw_eqtls$geneSymbol, 
                                      labels = 1:length(unique(gw_eqtls$geneSymbol))))

```

```{r get_sig_metabolites}
sig_metab_df <- metaboxcan %>% 
  dplyr::filter(pvalue < 0.05/nrow(metaboxcan)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene,gene_name,pvalue,zscore,best_gwas_p)

if (nrow(sig_metab_df) == 0) {
  print(paste0("NB: No Significant association at pvalue of ",0.05/nrow(metaboxcan),"(bonferroni sig). We are selecting the top 100 metabolite associations only."))
  sig_metab_df <- metaboxcan %>% 
    slice_min(pvalue, n = 100) %>% 
    dplyr::select(gene,gene_name,pvalue,zscore,best_gwas_p) 
    #dplyr::rename(metabolite_index=gene, Metabolite=gene_name) 
}

sig_metab_df <- paths_db %>% dplyr::select(metabolite = Metabolite.ID, 
                                    metabolite_name = Biochemical.name, HMDB) %>%
  inner_join(sig_metab_df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name), by = c("metabolite" = "gene")) %>%
  distinct(metabolite, .keep_all = TRUE)
```

```{r gw_mqtl, echo=FALSE}

# get the metabolites qtls
m_con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), metabolite_qtls_file)
metabo_db <- tbl(m_con,"snp2metabo")
gw_mqtls <- metabo_db %>% 
  dplyr::filter(metabolite %in% local(sig_metab_df$metabolite)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(metabolite,rsid,chr, pos, pvalue) %>% 
  collect()
dbDisconnect(m_con)

#TODO metabo liftover if not hg38
# liftover coordinates
if (! is.null(l_chain)) {
  # check if we have snps
  if(nrow(gw_mqtls != 0)) {
    gw_mqtls <- my_liftover(gw_mqtls,l_chain) %>% 
      mutate(study_id="snp2metabo")
    # format and add colour
    gw_mqtls$color <- as.character(factor(gw_mqtls$metabolite, 
                                      labels = 1:length(unique(gw_mqtls$metabolite))))
  }
} else {
  # format and add colour
  gw_mqtls <- gw_mqtls %>% mutate(study_id="snp2metabo")
  gw_mqtls$color <- as.character(factor(gw_mqtls$metabolite, 
                                      labels = 1:length(unique(gw_mqtls$metabolite))))
}

```

```{r summary_stats, echo=FALSE}
# TODO Ensure the sumstats are hg38
# Load the data
con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), summary_stat_file)
trait_db <- tbl(con,"summary_stats")
summary_stats <- trait_db %>% 
  dplyr::select(variant_id,chromosome, position, pvalue) %>% 
  collect()
dbDisconnect(con)

gw_stats <- summary_stats %>% 
  dplyr::rename(rsid=variant_id,pos=position,chr=chromosome) %>% 
  mutate(study_id="snp2trait")
```

```{r all-plot, echo=FALSE, out.width='99%'}
# gather ss. genes and metabo for a manhattan
comb_df <- gw_eqtls %>% 
  bind_rows(gw_mqtls ) %>% 
  bind_rows(gw_stats) 

comb_df$levs <- factor(comb_df$study_id,levels=c('snp2gene','snp2metabo', 'snp2trait'))

comb_df %>% 
  mutate(pvalue = ifelse(pvalue < 1e-30,1e-30,pvalue)) %>% 
  fast_manhattan(., build = "hg38", speed = "fast", pointsize = 2.1, pixels = c(1000,500)) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(5e-08), linetype =2, color ="deeppink") + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(1e-5), linetype =2, color ="grey") + 
  facet_wrap(~levs, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  ggplot2::theme_classic() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())
```


# Gene level results
## Top `r ntop` genes associated with `r phenotype_name`
The top gene associations are determined from the SMultiXcan results
```{r top_gene_assoc, echo=FALSE}

anno_gene <- fread("../data/annot_gene.txt")
#Top associations
corrected_gene_df <- sig_gene_df %>% 
  slice_min(pvalue, n = ntop) %>% 
  arrange(pvalue) %>% 
  mutate(pvalue = format(pvalue, scientific= T, digits=2)) %>% 
  mutate_if(is.numeric, format, digits=2) 

cytoband_data <- corrected_gene_df %>% 
  mutate(gene = gsub("\\..*","",gene)) %>% 
  addband2gene(.,"gene") %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, gene_name, cytoband, pvalue,z_mean, t_i_best)

DT::datatable(cytoband_data,
    options = list(
    scrollY = 500,  
    paging = FALSE  
  ))
```

The total number of significant genes associated with `r phenotype_name` is 
`r nrow(sig_gene_df)`, _p_ = `r 0.05/nrow(multixcan)` (bonferroni corrected).

## Manhattan plot of the multixcan results
```{r genes_manhattan, echo=FALSE, out.width='99%'}
# Load gene annotation file
annot <- fread(annotation_file)
gwas_data <- multixcan %>%
  dplyr::select(gene,gene_name,pvalue) %>% 
  inner_join(annot %>% dplyr::select(gene_id,chr,start,end), by = c("gene"="gene_id")) %>% 
  dplyr::rename(pos=start) %>% mutate(chr= paste0("chr",chr)) %>% 
  mutate(color = ifelse(pvalue < 0.05/nrow(multixcan),"red",NA))

fast_manhattan(gwas_data, build='hg19', speed = "slow") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05/nrow(multixcan)), 
             linetype =2, color ="blue") + # suggestive significance line
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = . %>% group_by(chr) %>% # ggrepel to avoid overplotting
                             top_n(1, -pvalue) %>% # extract highest y values
                             dplyr::slice(1) %>% # if there are ties, choose the first one
                             dplyr::filter(pvalue <= 5e-08), # filter for significant ones 
                             aes(label=gene_name), color =1) # add top rsid
```

## QQ plot plot of the multixcan results
```{r genes_qqplot, echo=FALSE}
fast_qq(pvalue = gwas_data$pvalue, speed = "slow", pointsize = 2)
```

## Loci with most gene significant associations
Using the LD blocks we are going to find the distribution of the genes across the LD blocks defined by [Berisa and Pickrell](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4731402/) 
for the `r nrow(sig_gene_df)` significant associations. We identify the lead gene in each of the loci and all the genes in that loci which are associated with `r phenotype_name`
```{r get-loci, echo=FALSE}
# load ldblocks
ldblocks <- fread(ldblocks_file, data.table = F)

## Add coords
genes <- sig_gene_df %>% 
  left_join(annot, by = c("gene" = "gene_id","gene_name" = "gene_name")) 

# check if df exists and remove it
if (exists('all_loci')) {rm(all_loci)}
for (n in 1:nrow(genes)){
  # extract genename, start and end
  gene_name <- as.character(genes[n,"gene_name"])
  gene_chr <- paste0("chr",genes[n,"chr"])
  gene_start <- as.numeric(genes[n,"start"])
  gene_end <- as.numeric(genes[n,"end"])
  
  locus <- ldblocks %>% 
    filter(chr == gene_chr) %>%
    filter(gene_start >= start & gene_end <= stop) %>% 
    mutate(locus_name = paste0(chr,"_",start,"_",stop)) %>% 
    dplyr::rename(locus_start=start,locus_end=stop) %>% 
    mutate(gene_name =gene_name, gene_start=gene_start,gene_end=gene_end)
  
  # create a data frame with info
  if (exists('all_loci') && is.data.frame(get('all_loci'))) {
    all_loci <- rbind(all_loci,locus)
  } else {
    all_loci <- locus
  }
}

# get top gene for each locus
locus_top_gene <- all_loci %>%
  left_join(genes %>% dplyr::select(gene_name,pvalue,z_mean), by = "gene_name") %>% 
  dplyr::rename(top_gene=gene_name,locus_chr=chr) %>% 
  group_by(locus_name) %>% mutate(n_genes = n()) %>% 
  slice_min(pvalue, n = 1) %>% 
  arrange(pvalue)

# get all the genes in each locus
all_genes_in_loci <- all_loci %>% group_by(locus_name) %>% 
  summarize(genes_in_locus = paste(sort(unique(gene_name)),collapse=", "))

# create a summary table
locus_summary <- locus_top_gene %>% 
  left_join(all_genes_in_loci, by = "locus_name") %>% 
  dplyr::select(locus_name,top_gene,pvalue,z_mean,n_genes,genes_in_locus,locus_chr,locus_start,locus_end) %>% 
  arrange(pvalue) %>% 
  mutate(pvalue = format(pvalue, scientific= T, digits=2)) %>% 
  mutate_if(is.numeric, format, digits=2)

DT::datatable(locus_summary,
    options = list(
    scrollY = 500,
    scrollX = TRUE,  
    paging = FALSE  
  ))
```

## Distribution of loci by chromosome
A quick look at which chromosome has the most loci associated with `r phenotype_name` based on the genes significantly associated with it.
```{r loci_summary, echo=FALSE}
chr_dist <- locus_summary %>% group_by(locus_chr) %>% mutate(loci_count = n()) %>%
  dplyr::select(locus_chr,loci_count) %>% distinct() %>% arrange(desc(loci_count)) %>% 
  left_join(locus_summary %>% group_by(locus_chr) %>% 
              summarize(loci_in_chr = paste(sort(unique(locus_name)),collapse=", ")))

DT::datatable(chr_dist,
    options = list(
    scrollY = 500,  
    paging = FALSE  
  ))
```


## Locus zoom plot of the top 5 significant loci
The plots below show the locus zoom plots for the ld blocks that contain the top genes associated with `r phenotype_name`. The metabolite qtls associated with the top gene are also selected and the SNPs within the region are plotted. The pink line shows the genome-wide significance line (5e-08) while the grey one shows suggestive significance line (1e-5).

```{r locus_plot, echo=FALSE}
## add gene annotations
gene_metabolite_df <- gene_metabolite_df %>% 
  left_join(annot %>% dplyr::select(gene_id,gene_name), by = c("gene"="gene_id"))

if(length(locus_summary$locus_name) >= 5){
  pl_loci <- unique(locus_summary$locus_name)[1:5]
} else {
  pl_loci <- unique(locus_summary$locus_name)
}
out = NULL
for (ld_locus in pl_loci){
  out = c(out, knit_expand('./ld_locus_plot.Rmd'))
}

```
`r paste(knit(text = out), collapse = '\n')`

# Metabolite level results

## Top `r ntop` metabolites associated with `r phenotype_name`
The top metabolite associations are determined from the SMetaboXcan results. Significant metabolites are selected after bonferroni correction (pvalue set at 0.05/total_metabolites). The metabolites are then ordered in descending value of -log10(p).

```{r top_metabo_assoc, echo=FALSE}
# Top associations
corrected_metab_df <- sig_metab_df %>% 
  slice_min(pvalue, n = ntop) %>% 
  arrange(pvalue) %>% 
  mutate(pvalue = format(pvalue, scientific= T, digits=2)) %>% 
  mutate_if(is.numeric, format, digits=2)

DT::datatable(corrected_metab_df,
    options = list(
    scrollY = 500,  
    paging = FALSE  
  ))
```


The total number of significant metabolites associated with `r phenotype_name` is 
`r nrow(sig_metab_df)`, _p_ < `r 0.05/nrow(metaboxcan)` (bonferroni corrected)

## Metabolite summary by super and sub pathway
```{r pathway_summary, echo=FALSE,warning=FALSE}
# Acat function
acat = function(pvec) 
{
  TT = sum( tan( (0.5 - pvec) *pi ) )
  .5 - atan(TT / length(pvec)) / pi
}

pathway_metabo <- paths_db %>% 
  dplyr::select(Metabolite.ID,Biochemical.name, SUB_PATHWAY, SUPER_PATHWAY) %>% 
  dplyr::rename(metabolite = Metabolite.ID, metabolite_name = Biochemical.name) %>% 
  right_join(metaboxcan %>% 
               dplyr::select(gene,zscore, pvalue) %>% 
               mutate(gene = gsub("\\..*","",gene)), 
             by = c("metabolite" = "gene")) %>% 
  mutate(SUB_PATHWAY = ifelse(SUB_PATHWAY == "","Undetermined",SUB_PATHWAY)) %>% 
  mutate(SUPER_PATHWAY = ifelse(SUPER_PATHWAY == "","Undetermined**",SUPER_PATHWAY)) %>% 
  mutate(SUB_PATHWAY = ifelse(SUB_PATHWAY == "UN","Undetermined",SUB_PATHWAY)) %>% 
  mutate(SUPER_PATHWAY = ifelse(SUPER_PATHWAY == "UN","Undetermined**",SUPER_PATHWAY)) %>% 
  mutate(SUB_PATHWAY = ifelse(is.na(SUB_PATHWAY),"Undetermined",SUB_PATHWAY)) %>% 
  mutate(SUPER_PATHWAY = ifelse(is.na(SUPER_PATHWAY),"Undetermined**",SUPER_PATHWAY)) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(SUB_PATHWAY != "Undetermined")
  

pathway_summary <- pathway_metabo %>% group_by(SUB_PATHWAY) %>% 
  transmute(super_pathway = SUPER_PATHWAY, mean_zscore = mean(zscore), 
            min_zscore = min(zscore),max_zscore = max(zscore),
            acat_pvalue = acat(pvalue),
            no_of_metabolites = n(),
            metabolites = paste(metabolite_name[1:10],collapse=", ")) %>% distinct() %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(metabolites = str_remove_all(metabolites, ", NA")) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(mean_zscore, min_zscore, max_zscore), ~round(., 2)) %>% 
  arrange(acat_pvalue) %>%  dplyr::rename(sub_pathway = SUB_PATHWAY)

DT::datatable(pathway_summary %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(acat_pvalue = format(acat_pvalue, scientific= T, digits=2)),
    options = list(
    scrollY = 500,  # Set the scroll height to 600 pixels
    scrollX = TRUE,
    paging = FALSE  # Disable pagination if you want all rows in the scrollable area
  ))
```

## Distribution of metabolite sub-pathway pvalues(acat)
The black line indicates the bonferroni significance threshold.
```{r pathway-qqunif, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}

## source qqunif from github
#devtools::source_gist("38431b74c6c0bf90c12f")
qqunif = 
  function(p,BH=T,CI=T,mlog10_p_thres=30,...)
  {
    ## thresholded by default at 1e-30
    p=na.omit(p)
    nn = length(p)
    xx =  -log10((1:nn)/(nn+1))
    
    p_thres = 10^{-mlog10_p_thres}
    if( sum( p < p_thres) )
    {
      warning(paste("thresholding p to ",p_thres) )
      p = pmax(p, p_thres)
    }
    plot( xx,  -sort(log10(p)),
          xlab=expression(Expected~~-log[10](italic(p))),
          ylab=expression(Observed~~-log[10](italic(p))),
          cex.lab=1.4,mgp=c(2,1,0),
          ... )
    abline(0,1,col='gray')
    if(BH)
    {
      abline(-log10(0.05),1, col='orange',lty=1)
      abline(-log10(0.10),1, col='orange',lty=2)
      abline(-log10(0.25),1, col='orange',lty=3)
      legend('topleft', c("FDR = 0.05","FDR = 0.10","FDR = 0.25"),
             col=c('orange','orange','orange'),lty=1:3, cex=1)
      abline(h=-log10(0.05/nn)) ## bonferroni
    }
    if(CI)
    {
      ## create the confidence intervals
      c95 <- rep(0,nn)
      c05 <- rep(0,nn)
      ## the jth order statistic from a
      ## uniform(0,1) sample
      ## has a beta(j,n-j+1) distribution
      ## (Casella & Berger, 2002,
      ## 2nd edition, pg 230, Duxbury)
      ## this portion was posted by anonymous on
      ## http://gettinggeneticsdone.blogspot.com/2009/11/qq-plots-of-p-values-in-r-using-ggplot2.html
      
      for(i in 1:nn)
      {
        c95[i] <- qbeta(0.95,i,nn-i+1)
        c05[i] <- qbeta(0.05,i,nn-i+1)
      }
      
      lines(xx,-log10(c95),col='gray')
      lines(xx,-log10(c05),col='gray')
    }
  }

## to add other qqplots on top of current plot
qqpoints = 
function(p,BH=T,mlog10_p_thres=30,...)
{
  ## thresholded by default at 1e-30
  p=na.omit(p)
  nn = length(p)
  xx =  -log10((1:nn)/(nn+1))
  
  p_thres = 10^{-mlog10_p_thres}
  if( sum( p < p_thres) )
  {
    warning(paste("thresholding p to ",p_thres) )
    p = pmax(p, p_thres)
  }
  nn = length(p)
  xx =  -log10((1:nn)/(nn+1))
  points( xx,  -sort(log10(p)), ... )
}

## to use with dplyr's pipes

qqunif.pipe = function(data,...){
  pval = data$pval
  qqunif(pval,...)
}

qqunif.compare = function(pvec1,pvec2,pthres=NULL,BF2=FALSE,col2='blue',cex2=1,...)
{
  pvec1 = na.omit(pvec1)
  pvec2 = na.omit(pvec2)
  if(!is.null(pthres)) {pvec1 = pmax(pvec1,pthres); pvec2 = pmax(pvec2,pthres)}
  qqunif(pvec1,...)
  qqpoints(pvec2,col=col2,pch='+',cex=cex2)
  if(BF2) abline(h=-log10(0.05/length(pvec2)),col='blue',lw=2)
}

## testing
qqunif_maxp = 
  function(p,BH=T,CI=T,mlog10_p_thres=30,maxp=1,...)
  {
    ## thresholded by default at 1e-30
    p=na.omit(p)
    nn = length(p)
    xx =  -log10( (1:nn)*maxp / (nn+1) )
    
    p_thres = 10^{-mlog10_p_thres}
    if( sum( p < p_thres) )
    {
      warning(paste("thresholding p to ",p_thres) )
      p = pmax(p, p_thres)
    }
    plot( xx,  -sort(log10(p)),
          xlab=expression(Expected~~-log[10](italic(p))),
          ylab=expression(Observed~~-log[10](italic(p))),
          cex.lab=1.4,mgp=c(2,1,0),
          ... )
    abline(0,1,col='gray')
    if(BH)
    {
      abline(-log10(0.05),1, col='red',lty=1)
      abline(-log10(0.10),1, col='orange',lty=2)
      abline(-log10(0.25),1, col='yellow',lty=3)
      legend('topleft', c("FDR = 0.05","FDR = 0.10","FDR = 0.25"),
             col=c('red','orange','yellow'),lty=1:3, cex=1)
      abline(h=-log10(0.05/nn)) ## bonferroni
    }
    if(CI)
    {
      ## create the confidence intervals
      c95 <- rep(0,nn)
      c05 <- rep(0,nn)
      ## the jth order statistic from a
      ## uniform(0,1) sample
      ## has a beta(j,n-j+1) distribution
      ## (Casella & Berger, 2002,
      ## 2nd edition, pg 230, Duxbury)
      ## this portion was posted by anonymous on
      ## http://gettinggeneticsdone.blogspot.com/2009/11/qq-plots-of-p-values-in-r-using-ggplot2.html

      for(i in 1:nn)
      {
        c95[i] <- qbeta(0.95,i,nn-i+1)*maxp ## CHECK THIS IS RIGHT
        c05[i] <- qbeta(0.05,i,nn-i+1)*maxp
      }

      lines(xx,-log10(c95),col='gray')
      lines(xx,-log10(c05),col='gray')
    }
  }

qqunif(pathway_summary$acat_pvalue)
```

## Ranked pathways association with `r phenotype_name`
The pathways are coloured by the super pathway they belong to, the grey line represents 
the bonferroni significance cut off. 
```{r pathway_assoc, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE, out.width='99%'}
pathway_summary %>% head(30) %>% 
   mutate(sub_pathway = str_replace(sub_pathway, "Metabolism","M")) %>% 
   mutate(log10p=-log10(acat_pvalue)) %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x = reorder(sub_pathway, log10p),log10p, fill = super_pathway) ) + 
    geom_bar(stat="identity") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05/nrow(pathway_summary)),col='gray' ,size=1)  +
    labs(x = "Sub pathway", fill = "Super Pathway") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8,face = "plain"),
          axis.title = element_text(size =12)) +
    coord_flip() + 
    ggtitle(glue::glue("pathway associations"))

```

## Top 20 pathways associated with `r phenotype_name`
The pathways are colored based if they passed the bonferroni correction significance 
set at `r 0.05/nrow(pathway_summary)`. The grey lines represent significance cut off 
for the z-scores.
```{r top-path-plot, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, out.width='99%'}
## boxplot by subpathway
zthres = abs( qnorm(0.05/nrow(pathway_metabo) / 2 ) ) ## BF sig Z score cutoff

## plot top 20 pathways's metabolite zscores
pathway_metabo %>% 
  dplyr::rename(sub_pathway=SUB_PATHWAY,super_pathway=SUPER_PATHWAY) %>% 
  inner_join(pathway_summary %>% 
               mutate(bf_sig_path= acat_pvalue<0.05/nrow(pathway_summary)) %>%  
               arrange(acat_pvalue) %>% 
               head(20) %>% ## select top 20 by pathway p-value acat_pvalue
               dplyr::select(sub_pathway,mean_zscore,acat_pvalue,bf_sig_path),
             by=c("sub_pathway"="sub_pathway")) %>%
  mutate(sub_pathway = str_replace(sub_pathway, "Metabolism","M")) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=reorder(sub_pathway,-acat_pvalue), zscore)) + 
  geom_violin() + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.8,aes(fill=bf_sig_path),width=0.2) + 
  geom_jitter(alpha=0.6) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = zthres*c(-1,1),color='gray',size=1,alpha=0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(y = "Zscore", x = "Sub pathway", fill = "Bonferroni Sig.")
```


```{r enrich-superpath, out.width='99%', fig.height=6.5, warning=FALSE}
qqunif_pathway = 
  function(p,BH=T,CI=T,mlog10_p_thres=30,...)
  {
    ## thresholded by default at 1e-30
    p=na.omit(p)
    nn = length(p)
    xx =  -log10((1:nn)/(nn+1))
    
    p_thres = 10^{-mlog10_p_thres}
    if( sum( p < p_thres) )
    {
      warning(paste("thresholding p to ",p_thres) )
      p = pmax(p, p_thres)
    }
    plot( xx,  -sort(log10(p)),
          xlab=expression(Expected~~-log[10](italic(p))),
          ylab=expression(Observed~~-log[10](italic(p))),
          cex.lab=0.9,mgp=c(2,1,0),
          ... )
    abline(0,1,col='gray')
    if(BH)
    {
      abline(-log10(0.05),1, col='red',lty=1)
      abline(-log10(0.10),1, col='orange',lty=2)
      abline(-log10(0.25),1, col='yellow',lty=3)
      legend('bottomright', c("FDR = 0.05","FDR = 0.10","FDR = 0.25"),
             col=c('red','orange','yellow'),lty=1:3, cex=0.4)
      abline(h=-log10(0.05/nn)) ## bonferroni
    }
    if(CI)
    {
      ## create the confidence intervals
      c95 <- rep(0,nn)
      c05 <- rep(0,nn)
      
      for(i in 1:nn)
      {
        c95[i] <- qbeta(0.95,i,nn-i+1)
        c05[i] <- qbeta(0.05,i,nn-i+1)
      }
      
      lines(xx,-log10(c95),col='gray')
      lines(xx,-log10(c05),col='gray')
    }
  }

## to add other qqplots on top of current plot

qqpoints=
function(p,BH=T,...)
{
  nn = length(p)
  xx =  -log10((1:nn)/(nn+1))
  points( xx,  -sort(log10(p)), ... )
}

## to use with dplyr's pipes

qqunif.pipe = function(data,...){
  pval = data$pval
  qqunif(pval,...)
}

qqunif.compare = function(pvec1,pvec2,pthres=NULL,...)
{
  pvec1 = na.omit(pvec1)
  pvec2 = na.omit(pvec2)
  if(!is.null(pthres)) {pvec1 = pmax(pvec1,pthres); pvec2 = pmax(pvec2,pthres)}
  qqunif(pvec1,...)
  qqpoints(pvec2,col='blue',pch='+')
  abline(h=-log10(0.05/length(pvec2)),col='blue',lw=2)
}

myqqsubpath = function(tempo,pathname,exact.match=FALSE) 
{
  ## tempo should be a data frame with pvalue and subpathway columns
  tempo %>% .[["pvalue"]] %>% qqunif_pathway()
  if(exact.match)
  {
    tempo2 = tempo %>% 
      filter(tolower(pathname)==tolower(subpathway) | 
               tolower(pathname)==tolower(superpathway)) 
  } else
  {
    tempo2 = tempo %>% 
      filter(grepl(pathname, subpathway, ignore.case = TRUE) | 
               grepl(pathname, superpathway, ignore.case = TRUE)) 
  }
  nsup=length(unique(tempo2$superpathway))
  nsub=length(unique(tempo2$subpathway))
  title(glue::glue("{pathname} (nsup= {nsup}; nsub={nsub})"),cex.main = 0.8)
  if(nrow(tempo2)>0) tempo2 %>% .[["pvalue"]] %>% qqpoints(pch='+', col=3) else
    warning("super/subpathway not found")
}

```

## Sub pathways enrichment plots
A plot of the top 9 sub pathways to determine which ones are enriched
```{r enrich-subpath, out.width='99%', fig.height=6, warning=FALSE}
#sub pathway
par(mfrow=c(3,3))
for(metabo in unique(pathway_summary$sub_pathway)[1:9]){
  metabo <- str_replace(metabo, "Metabolism","M")
  pathway_metabo %>% 
    dplyr::rename(subpathway=SUB_PATHWAY,superpathway=SUPER_PATHWAY) %>% 
    mutate(subpathway = str_replace(subpathway, "Metabolism","M")) %>%
    myqqsubpath(. ,metabo,exact.match = TRUE)
  
}
```


## Manhattan plot for the top 5 metabolites associated with `r phenotype_name`
A GWAS plot for each of the above metabolites associated with with the trait.The pink line shows the genome-wide significance line (5e-08) while the grey one shows suggestive significance line (1e-5).
```{r metabo-gwas_plots, echo=FALSE}

if(length(corrected_metab_df$metabolite) >= 5){
  pl_metabo <- corrected_metab_df$metabolite[1:5]
} else {
  pl_metabo <- corrected_metab_df$metabolite
}

for (metab in pl_metabo){
  # get the metname
  met_name <- corrected_metab_df[corrected_metab_df$metabolite == metab,]$metabolite_name
  # subset the data
  m_con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), metabolite_qtls_file)
  metabo_db <- tbl(m_con,"snp2metabo")
  fine_db <- tbl(m_con, "finemapping")
  all_mqtls <- metabo_db %>% 
    dplyr::filter(metabolite %in% local(metab)) %>% 
    dplyr::select(metabolite,rsid,chr, pos, pvalue) %>% 
    mutate(pvalue = ifelse(pvalue < 1e-30,1e-30,pvalue)) %>% 
    collect()
  fine_mqtls <- fine_db %>% 
    dplyr::filter(metabolite %in% local(metab)) %>% 
    collect()
  dbDisconnect(m_con)
  
  if (nrow(all_mqtls) >= 1) {
    # liftover genome co ordinates
    if (! is.null(l_chain)) {
      all_mqtls <- my_liftover(all_mqtls,l_chain)
    }
    mqtls <- all_mqtls %>% 
      mutate(chr = as.character(chr)) %>% 
      mutate(study_id="mqtl")
    # add colour to the fine mapped snps
    mqtls <- mqtls %>% left_join(fine_mqtls, by = c("metabolite", "rsid")) %>% 
      mutate(color = ifelse(! is.na(cs_cluster),"red", NA)) 
    
  } else {
    mqtls <- data.frame(chr = paste0("chr", 1), pos = 1200000,
                        pvalue= 0.01, metabolite = "none", cs_cluster = 1,rsid ="rs00")
  }
  
  # manhattan plot 
  p2 <- mqtls %>% 
  fast_manhattan(., build = "hg38", speed = "slow") + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(5e-08), linetype =2, color ="deeppink") + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(1e-5), linetype =2, color ="grey") + 
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = . %>% 
                             dplyr::filter(! is.na(cs_cluster)),
                             aes(label=rsid), color =1, size =2,max.overlaps = Inf) +
  labs(title = paste0(met_name," gwas plot")) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold.italic"))
  print(p2)
}

```

# Multiomic overview relationship to the trait
Combine gene level and metabolite level results to understand the connection between the molecular traits
```{r multiomic_view, echo=FALSE}
multi_dta <- gene_metabolite_df %>% dplyr::select(gene,Metabolite) %>% 
  dplyr::inner_join(paths_db %>% dplyr::select(Metabolite.ID,metabolite_name = Biochemical.name),
             by = c("Metabolite" = "Metabolite.ID")) %>% 
  dplyr::inner_join(metaboxcan, by = c("Metabolite" = "gene")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, Metabolite, metabolite_name, metabo_pval=pvalue, metabo_zscore=zscore) %>% 
  dplyr::inner_join(multixcan %>% dplyr::select(gene,gene_name, gene_pval = pvalue), 
             by = c("gene" = "gene"))

multi_clusters <- multi_dta %>% group_by(Metabolite) %>%
  arrange(metabo_pval, gene_pval) %>%
  transmute(metabolite_name = metabolite_name,
            m2t_p = metabo_pval,
            g2t_acat_p = acat(gene_pval),
            no_of_genes = n(),
            genes = paste(gene_name[1:10],collapse=", ")) %>%
  distinct() %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(genes = str_remove_all(genes, ", NA")) %>%
  arrange(m2t_p,g2t_acat_p) %>%
  mutate(m2t_p = format(m2t_p, scientific= T, digits=2),
         g2t_acat_p = format(g2t_acat_p, scientific= T, digits=2))

# view
DT::datatable(multi_clusters,
    options = list(
    scrollY = 500,  # Set the scroll height to 500 pixels
    paging = FALSE  # Disable pagination if you want all rows in the scrollable area
  ))
```

# Network analysis and candidate clusters
```{r network, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
source("./net.R")
met_info <- paths_db

# get scores table
net.scores <- metaboxcan %>% dplyr::select(gene,zscore,pvalue) %>% 
  mutate(gene = str_replace(gene,"met", "M")) %>% 
  mutate(b_sig =ifelse(pvalue < 0.05/nrow(.),"yes","no")) %>% 
  bind_rows(multixcan %>% dplyr::select(gene,z_mean,pvalue) %>%
              dplyr::rename(zscore = z_mean) %>% 
              mutate(gene = gsub("\\..*","",gene)) %>% 
              mutate(b_sig =ifelse(pvalue < 0.05/nrow(.),"yes","no"))) %>% 
  distinct()

# select significant nodes
ss <- top_selector(net.scores,m.network)

# run analysis
lc <- get_community(ss,m.network)
n.edges <- plt_edges(ss,m.network)
n.nodes <- plt_nodes(lc,net.scores,gene_info,met_info)
nn = n.nodes %>% filter((id %in% n.edges$from) | (id %in% n.edges$to)) %>%
  group_by(id) %>% dplyr::slice(1) %>% ungroup()
visNetwork(nn, n.edges) %>%
  visNodes(shadow = list(enabled = T, size = 5, x = 5, y =5),
           borderWidthSelected =5) %>%
  visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled =TRUE, degree = 1),
             nodesIdSelection = TRUE, selectedBy = "group") %>%
  visPhysics( solver = "barnesHut",barnesHut = list(gravity = -5000) ) %>%
  visIgraphLayout(randomSeed = 123,type = "full",
                  layout = "layout_with_kk")

## Save network
network <- visNetwork(nn, n.edges, width = "100%",height = 1200) %>%
  visNodes(shadow = list(enabled = T, size = 5, x = 5, y =5),
           borderWidthSelected = 5) %>%
  visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled =TRUE, degree = 1),
             nodesIdSelection = TRUE, selectedBy = "group") %>%
  visPhysics( solver = "barnesHut",barnesHut = list(gravity = -5000) ) %>%
  visIgraphLayout(randomSeed = 123,type = "full",
                  layout = "layout_with_kk")

n.dir = glue::glue("{out_dir}/nets")
if(!dir.exists(n.dir)){ dir.create(n.dir)}
visSave(network, file = glue::glue("{n.dir}/{phenotype_name}_network.html"))

```

## Clusters ranked by their pvalues
```{r clusters, echo=FALSE}
# tables
clusters <- get_clusters(n.nodes)
#main_neighbors <- get_neighbors(m.network,ss,1)

DT::datatable(clusters,
    options = list(
    scrollY = 500,  
    paging = FALSE  
  ))
```


## Locus zoom plot for gene with highest activity of the activity to trait and metabolites
Get a gene which has association with both the trait and the metabolites. Find all the metabolites associated with the gene and gene rate a locus zoom plot for the ld region where the gene is located.

```{r best-overlap}
## get best overlap
all_gene_assoc <- gene_metabolite_df %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene,Metabolite,pvalue) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(gene %in% sig_gene_df$gene) %>%
  left_join(sig_gene_df %>% dplyr::select(gene,gene_name,pvalue,t_i_best) %>%
              dplyr::rename(gene_trait_pval =pvalue),by = "gene")

all_met_assoc <- gene_metabolite_df %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene,Metabolite,pvalue) %>%  
  dplyr::filter(Metabolite %in% sig_metab_df$metabolite) %>% 
  left_join(sig_metab_df %>% dplyr::select(metabolite, pvalue) %>%
              dplyr::rename(met_trait_pval = pvalue), by = c("Metabolite" = "metabolite")) 
  

best_overlap <- all_gene_assoc %>% 
  inner_join(all_met_assoc) %>% 
  dplyr::rename(gene_met_pval = pvalue) %>% 
  mutate(gene_met_pval = -log10(gene_met_pval)) %>%
  mutate(gene_trait_pval = -log10(gene_trait_pval)) %>%
  mutate(met_trait_pval = -log10(met_trait_pval)) %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, format, digits=4)


if (nrow(best_overlap) == 0) {
  #print("Best overlap between genes and metabolites not detected. No plot")
} else {
  #best_eqtls <- py$best_df
  if(exists("best_eqtls") && is.data.frame(get('best_eqtls')) && nrow(best_eqtls) > 0){
    best_eqtls <- best_eqtls %>% 
      dplyr::select(snpId,chromosome,pos,pValue,geneSymbol) %>% 
      dplyr::rename(rsid=snpId,chr=chromosome,pvalue=pValue) %>% 
      mutate(rsid = sapply(rsid, toString)) %>% 
      mutate(pvalue = ifelse(pvalue < 1e-30,1e-30,pvalue)) %>%
      dplyr::distinct() %>% 
      mutate(study_id="snp2gene")
  } else {
    best_eqtls <- data.frame(chr = paste0("chr",1), pos = 1200000,
                        pvalue= 0.01, geneSymbol = "none",study_id="snp2gene")
  }
  
  best_overlap <- best_overlap %>% 
    left_join(gene_metabolite_df %>% dplyr::select(Metabolite, metabolite_index), 
              by = c("Metabolite"),relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
    dplyr::distinct()
  
  # collate the data
  m_con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), metabolite_qtls_file)
  metabo_db <- tbl(m_con,"snp2metabo")
  best_mqtls <- metabo_db %>% 
    dplyr::filter(metabolite %in% local(best_overlap$metabolite)) %>% 
    dplyr::select(metabolite,rsid,chr, pos, pvalue) %>% 
    collect()
  dbDisconnect(m_con)
  
  
  if (nrow(best_mqtls) >= 1) {
    # liftover coordinates
    if (! is.null(l_chain)) {
      best_mqtls <- my_liftover(best_mqtls,l_chain)
    }
    best_mqtls <- best_mqtls %>% 
      mutate(chr = as.character(chr)) %>% 
      mutate(study_id="snp2metabo")
  } else {
    best_mqtls <- data.frame(chr = paste0("chr",sel.chr), pos = locus_data$locus_start,
                        pvalue= 0.01, metabolite = "none",study_id="snp2metabo")
  }
  
  best_eqtls$color <- as.character(factor(best_eqtls$geneSymbol, 
                                        labels = 1:length(unique(best_eqtls$geneSymbol))))
  best_mqtls$color <- as.character(factor(best_mqtls$metabolite, 
                                        labels = 1:length(unique(best_mqtls$metabolite))))
}
```


```{r activity_locus_info}
# select the gene
ff <- best_overlap %>% group_by(gene_name) %>%  count() %>% 
  dplyr::rename(tot=n) %>% arrange(desc(tot)) 

# get information
best_gene = ff[1,"gene_name"]$gene_name
b_annot <- annot[annot$gene_name ==best_gene,]

all_loci <- ldblocks %>% 
    dplyr::filter(chr == paste0("chr",b_annot$chr)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(b_annot$start >= start & b_annot$end <= stop) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(locus_name = paste0(chr,"_",start,"_",stop)) %>% 
    dplyr::rename(locus_start=start,locus_end=stop) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(gene_name =b_annot$gene_name, gene_id = b_annot$gene_id,
                  gene_start=b_annot$start,gene_end=b_annot$end)

if(nrow(all_loci) == 0){
  start_loci <- ldblocks %>% 
      dplyr::filter(chr == paste0("chr",b_annot$chr)) %>%
      dplyr::filter(b_annot$start >= start & b_annot$start <= stop) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(locus_one = paste0(chr,"_",start,"_",stop)) %>% 
      dplyr::rename(locus_start=start) %>% dplyr::select(-c(stop))
  end_loci <- ldblocks %>% 
      dplyr::filter(chr == paste0("chr",b_annot$chr)) %>%  
      dplyr::filter(b_annot$end >= start & b_annot$end <= stop) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(locus_two = paste0(chr,"_",start,"_",stop)) %>% 
      dplyr::rename(locus_end=stop) %>% dplyr::select(-c(start)) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(gene_name =b_annot$gene_name, gene_id = b_annot$gene_id,
                  gene_start=b_annot$start,gene_end=b_annot$end)
  all_loci = start_loci %>% 
    inner_join(end_loci, by = "chr") %>% 
    mutate(locus_name = paste0(locus_one, "_and_", locus_two)) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-c(locus_one,locus_two))
  
  if (nrow(all_loci) == 1){
    print("This gene overlaps two ld regions")
  }
}

```

```{r activity_locus_plot, echo=FALSE}
out = "No plot generated"
if(nrow(all_loci) == 1){
  out = NULL
  out = c(out, knit_expand('./locus_activity.Rmd'))
} else {
  print("Couldn't determine the gene/locus with the most activity")
}
```
`r paste(knit(text = out), collapse = '\n')`

```{r save-res,message=FALSE,warning=FALSE,results=FALSE}

o.dir=glue::glue("{out_dir}/summaries")
if(!dir.exists(o.dir)){ dir.create(o.dir)}
phenotype_name = str_replace(phenotype_name, " ", "_")
of=glue::glue("{o.dir}/{phenotype_name}_summary.xlsx")
if(file.exists(of)){file.remove(of)}

OUT <- createWorkbook()

# smultixcan
sname <- "multixcan"
addWorksheet(OUT, sname)
writeData(OUT, sheet = sname, x = multixcan)

# smetaboxcan
metaboxcan <- paths_db %>% dplyr::select(metabolite = Metabolite.ID, 
                                    metabolite_name = Biochemical.name, HMDB) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(metaboxcan %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name), by = c("metabolite" = "gene")) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(metabolite, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
sname <- "metaboxcan"
addWorksheet(OUT, sname)
writeData(OUT, sheet = sname, x = metaboxcan)

# pathways
sname <- "pathways"
addWorksheet(OUT, sname)
writeData(OUT, sheet = sname, x = pathway_summary)

# multiomic clusters
sname <- "multiomic_clusters"
addWorksheet(OUT, sname)
writeData(OUT, sheet = sname, x = multi_clusters)

# network clusters
sname <- "network_clusters"
addWorksheet(OUT, sname)
writeData(OUT, sheet = sname, x = clusters)


saveWorkbook(OUT,of)


```
